---
title: "About & Contributing"
---

## Purpose

The Bioconductor Funding Hub is a community-maintained, public record of:

1. **Funding records** — grants and awards that have funded Bioconductor-related work, with enough
   detail (leads, team, dates, amount, objectives, outcomes) that future applicants can learn from
   precedent.
2. **Funding opportunities** — open or upcoming calls relevant to the project, so the community can
   discover them early and potentially collaborate on a joint application.

## Governance

This repository is community-maintained: anyone can propose an entry via pull request. Maintainers
review PRs for accuracy and tone; automated checks (see below) handle structural correctness so
reviewers don't have to.

## How contributions work

1. Copy the relevant template from [`_templates/`](https://github.com/Bioconductor/funding-hub/tree/main/_templates).
2. Rename it following `YYYY-funder-slug-lead-topic.md` and place it under the right year folder.
3. Fill in the frontmatter fields and the free-text sections.
4. Open a pull request.
5. A GitHub Actions check validates the frontmatter automatically. A maintainer then reviews for
   accuracy and merges.

Full details, including the field reference and the consent requirement for naming individuals, are
in [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/Bioconductor/funding-hub/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md).

## Data license

Content in this repository is published under [CC-BY-4.0](https://github.com/Bioconductor/funding-hub/blob/main/LICENSE) —
reuse it, just credit the source.
